Mirrors  >  Home  >  MPE Home  >  Th. List  >  eldifd Structured version   Visualization version   GIF version
Theorem eldifd 3915
Description: If a class is in one class and not another, it is also in their difference. One-way deduction form of eldif 3914. (Contributed by David Moews, 1-May-2017.)
Hypotheses
Ref Expression
eldifd.1 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝐴 ∈ 𝐵)
eldifd.2 ⊢ (𝜑 → ¬ 𝐴 ∈ 𝐶)
Assertion
Ref Expression
eldifd ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝐴 ∈ (𝐵 ∖ 𝐶))
Proof of Theorem eldifd
StepHypRef Expression
1 eldifd.1 . 2 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝐴 ∈ 𝐵)
2 eldifd.2 . 2 ⊢ (𝜑 → ¬ 𝐴 ∈ 𝐶)
3 eldif 3914 . 2 ⊢ (𝐴 ∈ (𝐵 ∖ 𝐶) ↔ (𝐴 ∈ 𝐵 ∧ ¬ 𝐴 ∈ 𝐶))
41, 2, 3sylanbrc 592 1 ⊢ (𝜑 → 𝐴 ∈ (𝐵 ∖ 𝐶))
Colors of variables: wff setvar class
Syntax hints:  ¬ wn 3   → wi 4   ∈ wcel 2142   ∖ cdif 3901
This theorem was proved from axioms:  ax-mp 5  ax-1 6  ax-2 7  ax-3 8  ax-gen 1815  ax-4 1829  ax-5 1930  ax-6 1987  ax-7 2028  ax-8 2144  ax-9 2152  ax-ext 2734
